# Course Overview {.unnumbered}

Welcome to our specialized course designed for PhD students keen on
mastering the art of statistical analysis in clinical research using R.
This course is meticulously crafted to bridge the gap between complex
clinical datasets and actionable insights through the power of
statistical tools and data visualization. Whether you are aiming to
enhance your data analytical skills, this course offers a comprehensive
pathway.

Leveraging the strengths of dplyr for efficient data manipulation and
ggplot2 for sophisticated data visualization, curriculum is structured
around practical, hands-on sessions with real-world clinical datasets.
These sessions are not just about learning the syntax but about
understanding how to apply these tools to uncover trends, patterns, and
insights in the given data sets. Once learnet these tools, you will be
able to apply them to your own research data, making your analysis more
efficient and insightful.

## What You Will Learn {.unnumbered}

-   Explore Publicly available clinical datasets.
-   Learn to do effective data documentation in R.
-   Understand and manage data using R's data structures.
-   Navigate through the installation and package management in R.
-   Import and visualize data.
-   How to get help.

## What You Will Not Learn {.unnumbered}

-   Advanced R programming.
-   Advanced statistical analysis.
-   Machine learning.
-   For these topics please look at our other upcoming courses.

## Why Choose R? {.unnumbered}

R is more than a programming language; it is a gateway to advanced data
analysis across various fields. Its superior statistical capabilities,
flexibility in handling large data sets, and a vast package ecosystem
make it an essential skill for researchers and professionals across all
domains. Whether you are in finance, healthcare, academia, or any field
that relies on data, R equips you with the power to make informed
decisions and uncover insights from data. By learning R, you are not
just gaining a skill, you are opening doors to a multitude of
opportunities in data-driven fields. If you are comfortable with Excel
and STATA, you will find R to be a natural progression but with more
power, flexibility and a larger community.

# Aims {#Aims}

1.  Introduce R and RStudio to learners.
2.  Guide through R and RStudio installation, including package
    management.
3.  Teach essential R syntax, operations, and R Markdown usage.
4.  Equip learners to manage R data structures for analysis.
5.  Actively engage learners throughout the course.

# Learning Objectives {#Learning-Objectives}

Upon completing this course, you'll gain proficiency in:

1.  R fundamentals and the RStudio IDE.
2.  Effortless installation of R and RStudio.
3.  Mastery of R syntax: variables, vectors, and operations.
4.  Utilization of R Markdown for documentation.
5.  Effective handling of vectors and exploration of data frames and
    lists.
6.  Navigation of R packages: installation, loading, and usage.
7.  Skills in data import, visualization, and accessing help in RStudio.

# Prerequisites {#Prerequisites}

A computer with a stable internet connection is required. Any operating
system (Windows, Mac, or Linux) is suitable, but Windows or Mac is
recommended for the best experience.

Install packages and software as per the instructions provided in the
below section.
